feedback.text: Here from the "stop killing games" campaign. Their demands seem plain common sense. EU legislation should be about protecting individual rights in all cases: protect free speech, crack down on corporate malpractice! The generalisation of subscription models is, I believe, much resented by the public -remember the "you will own nothing" quote? pretty unpopular... It should be curtailed, limited to the cases where transfer of ownership is demonstrably impossible or unrealistic. Courageous legislation could go a long way in securing the future of the union, while submission to private interests, careerism and, of course, wars, will destroy it in the end. Thanks for reading.
